model_name: GPT-3 (text-davinci-003, text-davinci-002, text-davinci-001, text-curie, text-babbage, text-ada)
notes: Original GPT-3 API release with multiple engine sizes (Ada, Babbage, Curie, Davinci). text-davinci-003 released later (~November 2022) as the final GPT-3.5-class instruct model before GPT-3.5 Turbo. text-davinci-002 released March 2022. Pricing evolved over time; original API was invite-only beta.

~137B parameters. Released May 2021 (paper); internal Google research model. Specialized for dialogue/conversation. Pre-trained on 1.56T words of public web documents and dialog data. Fine-tuned for quality, safety, and groundedness. Not released publicly; served internally and via limited API. Successor to Meena. Paper: "LaMDA: Language Models for Dialog Applications" (Google, 2022). Deprecated in favor of Gemini series.

Instruction-tuned variant of T5. Released October 2021 by Google Research. Sizes: Small (77M), Base (145M), Large (406M), XL (3B), XXL (11B). Paper: "Scaling Instruction-Finetuned Language Models" (Oct 2021). Apache 2.0 license. Flan-T5 XXL is the largest variant at ~11B parameters. Context window ~512 tokens. MMLU scores vary by size; XXL achieves competitive results on FLAN benchmarks. Included as it falls within the research lineage leading to subsequent Google models.

530B parameters. Jointly developed by NVIDIA and Microsoft. Released October 1, 2021. Transformer decoder (Megatron-LM architecture). Trained on 338B tokens (Pile dataset). Context window 2048 tokens. Not publicly released �?? served via Azure NDv4 instances. One of the largest dense models at the time. Demonstrated strong zero-shot and few-shot performance. Paper: "Using DeepSpeed and Megatron to Train Megatron-Turing NLG 530B" (Smith et al., 2022). Source: https://theresanaiforthat.com/model/megatron-turing-nlg/

280B parameters. Released December 2021 by DeepMind (pre-Google merger). Dense transformer. Context window 2048 tokens. MMLU: 74.8% (5-shot). Not publicly released. Important for establishing scaling laws. Paper: "Scaling Language Models: Methods, Analysis & Insights from Training Gopher" (Rae et al., 2021).
Codex model specialized for code completion. Free during beta, then discontinued.
Early GPT-3.5-class model using InstructGPT training.

70B parameters, trained on 1.4T tokens. Released March 29, 2022 by DeepMind. Introduced the "Chinchilla scaling laws" (optimal model size vs. training tokens). MMLU: 77.6% (5-shot). Outperformed Gopher (280B) and GPT-3 (175B) despite being smaller. Not publicly released. One of the most influential LLM research papers. Paper: "Training Compute-Optimal Large Language Models" (Hoffmann et al., 2022).

Same entry as above, emphasizing the Chinchilla scaling law contribution: models should be trained on ~20 tokens per parameter (not 2-3 as previously done). This insight reshaped LLM training across the industry. 2048 token context. Not released publicly.

540B parameters. Released April 4, 2022 by Google Research. Dense decoder-only transformer trained on 780B tokens. Context window 2048 tokens. MMLU: 79.3% (5-shot). Achieved breakthrough performance on hundreds of benchmarks. Trained on TPUv4 pods. Not publicly released. Paper: "PaLM: Scaling Language Modeling with Pathways" (Chowdhery et al., 2022).
13B parameters. Released May 3, 2022. Fully open. 2048 token context. Apache 2.0.

Full size range: 125M, 350M, 1.3B, 2.7B, 6.7B, 13B, 30B, 66B, 175B �?? all released May 3, 2022. GPT-3 architecture replica trained by Meta. 175B version required approved research access; smaller sizes fully open. Pre-trained on 180B tokens. Context window 2048 tokens. Apache 2.0 license for smaller sizes; OPT-175B required research agreement. Open-sourced training logbook and metaseq codebase. Trained on 992 A100-80GB GPUs. ~1/7th carbon footprint of GPT-3. Paper: "OPT: Open Pre-trained Transformer Language Models" (Zhang et al., 2022). Source: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2205.01068
30B parameters. Released May 3, 2022. Fully open. 2048 token context. Apache 2.0.
6.7B parameters. Released May 3, 2022. Fully open. 2048 token context.

66B parameters. Released May 3, 2022 alongside the full OPT family. Fully open weights (no access restrictions). Context window 2048 tokens. Apache 2.0 license. Part of Meta's first major open LLM release.

Released at Google I/O 2022. Improved version of LaMDA with better factual grounding. Opened to limited preview for developers. ~137B parameters. Part of Google's dialogue model research lineage leading to Gemini. Status: deprecated.

First version of CodeGeeX. 13B parameters. Trained on 850B tokens across 23 programming languages using 1,536 Ascend 910 AI processors. Open-sourced Sep 2022 with IDE extensions for JetBrains and VS Code.

Scientific language model. Full size range: 125M, 1.3B, 6.7B, 30B, 120B �?? all released November 15, 2022. Trained on 106B tokens of scientific data (48M papers, 360M citations). 120B version fit on single A100 node (80GB). Demo was withdrawn after 3 days (November 17, 2022) due to criticism over generating fabricated citations. Model weights remain available on Hugging Face. Apache 2.0 license. Notable as one of the first major "science LLMs." Source: https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s00146-024-02088-7
Best GPT-3 completions model before Turbo. Improved instruction following.

~20B parameters. Released December 2022 by Google Research. UL2 is an encoder-decoder model trained with a mixture of denoisers. Flan-UL2 is the instruction-tuned variant. Apache 2.0 license. Context window ~2048 tokens. Important as an open-source predecessor to PaLM-era models. Paper: "UL2: Unifying Language Learning Paradigms" (May 2022).
Released February 24, 2023. MMLU ~46.9%. Gated/research license. Weights were leaked, leading to widespread open-source fine-tuning.
Released February 24, 2023. MMLU ~57.8%. Gated access.
Largest Llama 1 variant. Released February 24, 2023. MMLU ~63.4%. Gated access.

Original LLaMA release. Released February 24, 2023. Sizes: 7B, 13B, 33B, 65B. All trained on 1T+ tokens (varying by size). Gated access (research license, weights leaked). 7B MMLU: ~35.1%. 2048 token context. LLaMA-13B matched or exceeded GPT-3 on many benchmarks despite being much smaller. Transformer architecture with pre-normalization, SwiGLU activations, and rotary positional embeddings. Catalyzed the open-source LLM revolution (Alpaca, Vicuna, etc.). Source: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.12178v1
First chat-optimized GPT-3.5 model. Pricing was later reduced to $0.50/$1.50 in March 2023, then further to $0.50/$1.50. Legacy variant deprecated in favor of gpt-3.5-turbo-0125.
Anthropic's first public release. Constitutional AI-based. No public MMLU or SWE-bench scores available. Pricing inferred from Claude 2.0 rates. Context window was ~100K tokens. Retired.
Streamlined, faster model with limited capabilities. Released alongside Claude 1.0. Claude Instant 1.2 released 2023-08-09.
Initial GPT-4 release with 8K context. Also available in 32K context variant. Multimodal (text + image). Later pricing reduced significantly.
32K context variant of GPT-4 base. Very expensive at launch.
Larger ChatGLM variants from 12B to 130B parameters. ChatGLM-130B was the cloud API model (~85% of ChatGPT performance). Only 6B was open-sourced. Larger variants were proprietary API-only.

First open-source ChatGLM model. 6B parameters, trained on 1T bilingual tokens. INT4 quantization fits in 6GB GPU. 10M downloads on HuggingFace. #1 on GitHub Trending Mar 2023. MMLU: ~40.6% (chat, zero-shot CoT). GSM8K: 4.82%. BBH: 18.73%. Apache-2.0 license. Distilled from GLM-130B.
Improved reasoning and code generation over Claude 1.0. Exact benchmark scores not publicly reported.
Second generation. 6B parameters. Based on ChatGLM2 architecture. Significant improvements in code generation.

Released at Google I/O 2023 (May 10, 2023). Multiple size variants: Gecko, Otter, Bison, Unicorn. Bison is the mid-size workhorse. MMLU 5-shot ~81.2% (Bison). Context window 8192 tokens. Pricing was $0.50/1M input, $1.00/1M output for Bison chat. Deprecated February 2025; replaced by Gemini series. PaLM 2 improved on multilingual, reasoning, and coding capabilities over PaLM. Not publicly weighted �?? closed API-only.
Largest PaLM 2 variant. MMLU ~83.6% (5-shot). Same release date as PaLM 2 Bison. Limited API availability. Deprecated with transition to Gemini.
Extended context variant. Later pricing dropped to $0.50/$1.50 for input/output with model updates.

Second generation. 6B parameters, trained on 1.4T bilingual tokens. 42% faster inference than ChatGLM-6B. Free commercial use. MMLU: +23% over ChatGLM-6B (45.5% chat, 47.9% base). C-Eval: 50.1% (chat). GSM8K: 28.05% (chat). BBH: 30.0%. #1 on HF Trending Jun-Jul 2023. FlashAttention + Multi-Query Attention.
32K context variant of ChatGLM2-6B. Same base model with extended context via YaRN/FlashAttention techniques.
Doubled context to 100K tokens. Stronger coding and analysis. Pioneered long-context processing. Performance degrades after ~70K tokens. Hallucination rate moderate.
Released July 18, 2023. MMLU ~54.8% (5-shot). 4096 token context. Llama 2 license.

Released July 18, 2023. MMLU ~69.6% (5-shot). 4096 token context. GQA for inference efficiency. API pricing (varies by provider): ~$0.70-$0.90/1M tokens. Llama 2 license. Pre-trained on 2T tokens, 3.3M GPU hours on A100-80GB. 539 tCO2eq (100% offset). Source: https://ai.meta.com/blog/future-of-ai-built-with-llama/

Released July 18, 2023. Sizes: 7B, 13B, 70B + Code Llama variants. All trained between January 2023 and July 2023. 7B MMLU: ~45.8% (5-shot). Context window 4096 tokens (double Llama 1). Llama 2 Chat versions fine-tuned with SFT + RLHF. Llama 2 license (custom commercial license with 700M user threshold). Pre-trained on 2T tokens. Grouped-Query Attention (GQA) used for 70B. Source: https://huggingface.co/meta-llama/Llama-2-7b
First multimodal model in Qwen family. 7B parameters. Processes image and text input. Tongyi Qianwen license. ```
Internally incubated throughout 2023. First LLM from ByteDance. Rebranded from Skylark to Doubao. ```
Early abab series model. Passed national first batch of large model service filings Aug 2023. Opened to public by end of Aug 2023. Exact specs unclear �?? precursor to abab6.
Initial release. 7B parameters. Training data: 2.2T tokens (v1), 2.4T (v2). 32 layers, 4096 hidden dim. Apache 2.0 license for some variants. ```
Chat-tuned variants using SFT and RLHF. Int4/Int8 quantization support added in October 2023. ```
Update to Claude Instant 1.1 with improved performance.

Code-specialized Llama 2. Released August 24, 2023. Sizes: 7B, 13B, 34B, 70B. Three variants per size: Code Llama (foundation), Code Llama - Python, Code Llama - Instruct. 16K token context (4x Llama 2). Infilling capabilities. HumanEval: 53.7% (70B Instruct). Llama 2 community license. Source: https://github.com/facebookresearch/codellama

Long-context variant of Llama 2. ~32K token context. Released September 2023 as research paper. Uses positional interpolation to extend context. Sizes: 7B, 13B, 70B. Paper: "Llama Long: Efficient Long-Context Inference with LLaMA" (Chen et al., 2023). Research release.
Instruct-tuned variant for completions API (not chat). Replaced text-davinci-003.
14B parameters. 3.0T training tokens. Released alongside Qwen-7B v2. ```

Mistral AI's debut model. 7.3B parameters. Apache 2.0 license. Popularized Grouped-Query and Sliding Window Attention. Outperformed Llama 2 13B. MATH 13.1%, MT-Bench 6.84, HumanEval 30.5%, MMLU 60.1%.
Instruction-tuned variant of Mistral 7B.

Third generation. Significant capability improvements. MMLU base: 61.4%. GSM8K: 72.3%. HumanEval: 58.5%. C-Eval: 69.0%. CMMLU: 67.5%. Custom 'ChatGLM3-6B License' (not standard Apache). Foundation for CodeGeeX3-6B.
128K context variant. For very long document processing.
32K context variant. Extended context for long-document tasks.
Third generation. Based on ChatGLM3-6B. Improved multilingual code generation.

First Nemotron release. November 2023. Enterprise-ready models via NVIDIA NeMo. 8B parameter base model plus chat-sft and chat-rlhf variants. Part of NVIDIA's NeMo framework. Nemotron-3 also included 22B and 43B variants. Gated access via NVIDIA AI Enterprise. Source: https://www.interconnects.ai/p/why-nvidia-builds-open-models-with

First DeepSeek model. 1.3B parameters, trained on 2T tokens (87% code, 13% natural language). Llama-like architecture. Open-sourced under DeepSeek License. Also available as 5.7B, 6.7B, 33B variants. HuggingFace: deepseek-ai/deepseek-coder-1.3b-base
33B parameter coding model �?? largest in Coder V1 family. Instruct variant achieves 73.8% on HumanEval, competitive with GPT-3.5 Turbo at the time.
6.7B parameter coding model. Base and Instruct variants. HumanEval: ~48% (Instruct). HuggingFace collection updated Feb 2024.

314B parameter Mixture-of-Experts model (25% active weights). First xAI model. Released as open-weight model on March 17, 2024 under Apache 2.0 license. Real-time access to X (Twitter) data. Knowledge cutoff Q3 2023.
Preview release at DevDay 2023. First GPT-4 with 128K context. Knowledge cutoff April 2023. Included vision capabilities.

First commercial AI model with 128K context window. Founded March 2023. Released October 2023 for closed beta, general public November 16, 2023. Focus on Chinese-language processing and long documents. Moonshot AI founded by Yang Zhilin (Tsinghua).
Doubled context to 200K tokens. Reduced hallucination rate. Tool use support added. Stable up to ~200K tokens. Knowledge cutoff January 1, 2023. Max output ~4K tokens.

Flagship 67B parameter model. Outperformed LLaMA-2 70B in reasoning, coding, math, and Chinese comprehension. MMLU: 71.3% base. GSM8K: 84.1% (chat). HumanEval: 73.8% (chat). BBH: 71.7% (chat). Major milestone establishing DeepSeek as a serious Chinese AI lab.

General-purpose LLM. 7B parameters. Trained on 2T tokens. Llama-like architecture with Pre-norm Transformer, RoPE, GQA. MMLU base: 48.2%, chat: 49.4%. GSM8K: 62.6% (chat). First general-purpose model from DeepSeek.
1.8B parameters. 2.2T training tokens. Efficient small model. BF16: 4.23GB GPU memory. ```
72B parameters. 3.0T training tokens. 64 layers. System prompt enhancement. 32K context length. ```
API ID: mistral-medium-2312. Original Mistral Medium model. Mid-tier enterprise model. Retired June 16, 2025.
46.7B total parameters (8 experts, 7B each). Sparse mixture-of-experts model. Apache 2.0 license. Outperformed GPT-3.5 on benchmarks. Released via torrent.

On-device model for Android smartphones. Released December 2023. Two variants: Nano-1 (text-only) and Nano-2 (multimodal). ~4K token context. Runs locally on Pixel and Samsung Galaxy devices. Not an API model �?? deployed via Android AICore.

First Gemini generation. Released December 13, 2023. 32K token context. MMLU 5-shot: 71.8%. Multimodal (text, image, audio). Retired February 18, 2025. Part of Gemini 1.0 announcement with Ultra and Nano. Pricing from early 2024: $0.50/1M input, $1.50/1M output. Source: https://docsbot.ai/models/compare/gemini-1-0-ultra/gemini-1-0-pro
Semantic text representation model for embeddings, search, and comparison. No output tokens (embedding model).

First MoE model. 16B total parameters with 2.8B active per token. Early MoE variant with shared and routed experts. MMLU: 45.0%. Paper: "Towards Ultimate Expert Specialization in Mixture-of-Experts Language Models" (arXiv:2401.06066, Jan 11, 2024).
First MoE architecture model from MiniMax. "Fully released" Jan 2024 on Open Platform. Used in WPS AI product for text generation and summarization.

First GLM-4 generation API model. 128K context. Multimodal capabilities (text, image, code). January 2024 launch. Improved to GLM-4(0520) and GLM-4 Air(0605). MMLU progression per ChatGLM paper: ~83.3%.

Multilingual LLM trained to 8T tokens. Released February 2024. Dense transformer. Minitron-4B pruned variant released July-September 2024. Paper: "Nemotron-4 15B Technical Report" (NVIDIA, 2024). Source: https://www.interconnects.ai/p/why-nvidia-builds-open-models-with
Requires under 2GB memory for inference. Strong edge deployment candidate. Apache 2.0. ```
Improved over Qwen-1.8B with better alignment and multilingual support. ```
Largest Qwen1.5 model. 32K context. MMLU-Pro: 52.1. ```
MMLU 5-shot approximately 68%. DROP: 75.6. ```
32K context. HumanEval: 78.1. ```
New size introduced in Qwen1.5. ```
32K context. IFEval: 91.2. Apache 2.0. ```
Context window: 8K for this size variant. BBH: 70.5. ```

Specialized math model. 7B parameters. Scored 51.7% on MATH benchmark �?? approaching Gemini-Ultra/GPT-4 performance at the time. Trained on 500B math-focused tokens. Based on Coder v1.5 architecture with GRPO RL and Process Reward Model. GSM8K: 88.2%. Major breakthrough for open-source math reasoning.

Largest Gemini 1.0 variant. Released February 8, 2024 (after initial December 2023 announcement). 32K token context. MMLU 5-shot: 83.7%. MMMU: 59.4% (0-shot). HumanEval: 74.4% (0-shot). Retired with transition to Gemini 1.5. Source: https://docsbot.ai/models/compare/gemini-1-0-ultra/gemini-1-0-pro

Announced February 15, 2024. Multimodal Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture. 1M token standard context, expandable to 2M tokens. MMLU 5-shot ~81.9%. Generally available May 23, 2024. Deprecated April 2025 in favor of Gemini 2.5 Pro. Pricing from early 2025: $3.50/1M input, $10.50/1M output. Source: https://www.techtarget.com/whatis/feature/Gemini-15-Pro-explained-Everything-you-need-to-know

First Gemma release. Released February 21, 2024. 2B parameters. Lightweight open model. Context window 8192 tokens. MMLU: ~42.3% (2B). Gemma license (permissive commercial). Runs on laptops/desktops. Source: https://www.techtarget.com/searchenterpriseai/definition/Gemma
Released February 21, 2024 alongside 2B. 7B parameters. MMLU: ~64.3%. Gemma license. Context window 8192 tokens. Source: https://www.techtarget.com/searchenterpriseai/definition/Gemma
Mistral's first flagship large model. 32K context (original), later expanded to 128K. MMLU 81.2% (5-shot), HellaSwag 89.2% (10-shot). Multilingual. Strong reasoning and coding. Retired June 16, 2025.
API ID: mistral-small-2402. Lightweight enterprise-grade model. Released alongside Mistral Large. Retirement date: 2025-06-16.

API ID: claude-3-opus-20240229. First multimodal Claude (vision input). Top benchmark scores at launch �?? MMLU 86.8%, GPQA 50.4%, GSM8K 95.0%. Swept benchmarks, clearing GPT-4 and Gemini 1.0 Ultra on most tests.
API ID: claude-3-sonnet-20240229. Balanced performance/cost model. Strong coding model for the generation.
Vision-language model. 1.3B parameters. Processes 384x384 images. Hybrid vision encoder. Paper: arXiv:2403.05525 (Mar 8, 2024). Open-sourced for academic and commercial use.

7B parameter VL model. Processes 1024x1024 images with hybrid vision encoder (SigLIP-L, SAM-B). Strong performance on visual QA, OCR, chart/document understanding. OCRBench: 456. Competed with larger models at the time.
API ID: claude-3-haiku-20240307. Fastest, cheapest Claude 3 tier. Near-instant responses. MMLU 75.2%.
First MoE model in Qwen family. 2.7B activated parameters. ```
Significant improvement over Grok-1. 128K context window. Stronger reasoning and coding. Math benchmarks improved substantially.
Vision-capable variant of Grok-1.5. First Grok model with image understanding. Can process documents, diagrams, photographs, and screenshots.
Updated Gemma 1 versions. Released April 5, 2024. Instruction-tuned variants with improved performance. Source: https://ai.google.dev/gemma/docs/releases

Code-specialized Gemma variants. Released April 9, 2024. 2B and 7B sizes. Optimized for code completion and generation. CodeGemma v1.1 released May 3, 2024. Source: https://ai.google.dev/gemma/docs/releases
Uses Griffin architecture (recurrent + attention). Released April 9, 2024. 9B variant released June 11, 2024. Source: https://ai.google.dev/gemma/docs/releases
General availability release of GPT-4 Turbo. Knowledge cutoff December 2023. Reduced hallucinations vs preview.
Trillion-parameter MoE model (exact count undisclosed). Deep optimization on MoE architecture. 200K context length. Part of abab 6.5 series launch.
Efficient variant of abab6.5. Processes nearly 30,000 characters per second. Same training techniques and data as abab6.5 but optimized for speed.
141B total parameters, 22B active. Sparse MoE model. Released via BitTorrent. Apache 2.0 license. Strong performance on multilingual tasks.

Released April 18, 2024. MMLU 5-shot: 82.0% (Instruct). 8K token context. Trained on 15T+ tokens. Llama 3 license. API pricing: ~$0.70/1M input, $0.90/1M output. HumanEval: 81.7% (0-shot). GPQA: 39.5% (0-shot). GSM-8K: 93.0% (8-shot CoT). Source: https://huggingface.co/meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-70B

Released April 18, 2024. Sizes: 8B and 70B (initial release). 8K token context (2x Llama 2). MMLU 5-shot: 68.4% (Instruct). Trained on 15T+ tokens (7x Llama 2). New tokenizer (128K vocab). Grouped-Query Attention (GQA). Llama 3 license. API pricing: ~$0.05/1M input, $0.08/1M output (varies by provider). HumanEval: 62.2% (8B), 81.7% (70B). Significant leap over Llama 2. Source: https://ai.meta.com/blog/meta-llama-3/

236B total parameters, 21B activated per token. MoE architecture with Multi-Head Latent Attention (MLA) and DeepSeekMoE. Trained on 8.1T tokens. Triggered China's AI price war �?? pricing at 1 RMB per million input tokens forced ByteDance, Tencent, Baidu, Alibaba to slash prices. C-Eval: 81.7%. CMMLU: 84.0%. GSM8K: 79.2%. Pricing at launch was 1 RMB input / 2 RMB output per million tokens. Exchange rate: ~7.25 RMB/USD �?? ~$0.14/$0.28.
15.7B total parameters, 2.4B activated. Smaller variant for efficient deployment. MLA + MoE architecture. GSM8K: 41.1% (base). MMLU chat: 55.7%.

"Omni" model �?? native multimodal (text, image, audio, video). 5x faster than GPT-4 Turbo. 50% cheaper. MMLU 88.7% (5-shot). GPT-4o-2024-08-06 introduced structured outputs. GPT-4o-2024-11-20 is the latest snapshot.

Vision-language model based on Gemma. Released May 14, 2024. 3B parameters. PaliGemma 2 (3B/10B/28B) released December 5, 2024. PaliGemma 2 mix released February 19, 2025. Source: https://ai.google.dev/gemma/docs/releases
Lightweight variant of Doubao. Cost-efficient. ```

Flagship Doubao model. Processing 2M Chinese characters (~1.25M tokens) for RMB 1. 99.3% cheaper than industry average. Ranked 2nd on FlagEval behind GPT-4. Daily token usage surpassed 4 trillion by late 2024. Exchange rate: 1 USD = 7.25 RMB (May 2024). ```
Specialized theorem-proving model. Focused on formal mathematics and proof assistant tasks. Paper published May 23, 2024.

Fast, cost-efficient version of 1.5 Pro. Released May 23, 2024 at Google I/O. 1M token context. Deprecated April 30, 2025. Users migrated to 2.5 Flash. Pricing: $0.35/1M input, $1.05/1M output. Source: https://www.gradually.ai/en/gemini-models/
22B parameters. Code-focused model, fluent in 80+ programming languages. Fill-in-the-middle completion. Text-only. Mistral Non-Production License.
Base 9B model. Foundation for GLM-4-9B-Chat and variants. June 5, 2024 open-source release.

Open-source chat model. 9B parameters. 128K context. Supports web browsing, code execution, function calling. Multilingual (26 languages). MMLU: 72.4%. GSM8K: 79.6%. MATH: 50.6%. HumanEval: 71.8%. C-Eval: 75.6%. Surpassed Llama-3-8B on most benchmarks. Berkeley Function Calling: 81.0% overall.
1M context length variant. ~2 million Chinese characters. Needle-in-haystack tested at 1M context. For ultra-long document processing.
Cost-effective API variant. Achieves comparable performance to GLM-4(0116) with lower latency and inference cost. Targeted at production deployments.
Fastest, lowest-cost API variant. Optimized for speed over maximum accuracy. MalayMMLU: 63.59%.

Multimodal variant. 9B parameters. Processes 1120x1120 images. Vision-language capabilities in Chinese and English. Outperformed GPT-4-turbo-2024-04-09, Gemini 1.0 Pro, Qwen-VL-Max on multimodal benchmarks.
GSM8K: 55.6. PIQA: 52.3. Apache 2.0. ```
HumanEval Python: 68.4. Apache 2.0. ```
MoE model. 57B total, 14B active parameters. Apache 2.0. ```
Licensed under Tongyi Qianwen (not Apache 2.0). Arena-Hard-Auto: 88.6. MT-Bench: 92.1. ```
AlpacaEval 2.0: 89.4 (Qwen2-7B-Instruct reference). Flagship Qwen2 model. ```
HumanEval: 73.9. GPQA Diamond: 82.5. MuSR: 81.3. Apache 2.0. ```

Major open release. 340B parameters. Released June 14, 2024. Dense decoder-only transformer. Trained on 9T tokens (70% English, 15% multilingual, 15% code). Context window 4096 tokens. Grouped-Query Attention (GQA). NVIDIA Open Model License (commercial use permitted). MMLU: ~76.0% (5-shot). BBH: competitive with Llama-3 70B, Mixtral 8x22B. Fits on single DGX H100 (8 GPUs) in FP8. Designed for synthetic data generation. Source: https://research.nvidia.com/publication/2024-06_nemotron-4-340b

Instruction-tuned Nemotron-4 340B. Released June 14, 2024. 98%+ synthetic data for alignment (only ~20K human-annotated examples). MMLU 0-shot: ~78.7%. HumanEval: 73.7% (0-shot). MBPP: 74.8% (0-shot). Multi-phase alignment: Code SFT �?? General SFT �?? DPO �?? RPO. Strong on RewardBench. NVIDIA Open Model License. Source: https://arxiv.org/html/2406.11704v1

Reward model released alongside Nemotron-4 340B. Top accuracy on RewardBench at publication. Surpassed proprietary models (GPT-4o-0513, Gemini 1.5 Pro-0514). Unusual open release of a reward model. NVIDIA Open Model License. Source: https://arxiv.org/html/2406.11704v1

236B total parameters, 21B activated. Extended from DeepSeek-V2 with additional 6T code-focused tokens. Supports 338 programming languages (up from 86). First open-source model to surpass 10% on SWE-bench. HumanEval: 90.2%, MBPP: 76.2%, LiveCodeBench: 43.4%. MATH: 75.7% �?? nearly matching GPT-4o. Competed with GPT-4 Turbo, Claude 3 Opus on code tasks.
16B total parameters, 2.4B activated. MMLU: 60.1%. More efficient variant for local deployment.
API ID: claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620. Landmark release �?? outperformed Claude 3 Opus on most benchmarks at 1/5 the price. Max output 8,192 tokens. MMLU 88.7%, GPQA 59.4%, HumanEval 64%, GSM8K 96.4%.

Released June 27, 2024 alongside 9B. 27B parameters. MMLU: ~75.2%. Context window 8192 tokens. Pricing (API): $0.65/1M input, $0.65/1M output. Source: https://blog.galaxy.ai/compare/gemma-2-27b-it-vs-gemma-2-9b-it

Second generation Gemma. Released June 27, 2024. Major architecture improvements. 9B and 27B sizes. 2B added July 31, 2024. MMLU 9B: ~71.3%. Context window 8192 tokens. Pricing (API): $0.06/1M input, $0.06/1M output. Source: https://docsbot.ai/models/gemma-2-9b

Fourth generation. 9B parameters. Continually trained on GLM-4-9B. Supports code completion, generation, code interpreter, web search, function call, repository-level Q&A. 128K context. Most powerful code model under 10B parameters. Competitive with CodeLlama 70B and DeepSeek-Coder-33B. BigCodeBench and NaturalCodeBench strong results.
STEM-focused model specialized for mathematical reasoning. MATH 56.6%, MMLU 63.47%. Apache 2.0 license.
12B parameters. Robust multilingual open-source model. Replaced Mistral 7B as Mistral's recommended base model. Apache 2.0 license.
Highly cost-efficient small model. 128K context, 16K max output. MMLU 82.0% (5-shot). Outperforms GPT-3.5 Turbo at fraction of cost.
7.3B parameter Mamba-based model. Linear time inference, theoretically infinite sequence lengths. Apache 2.0 license. Optimized for quick code responses.

Largest open LLM at release. 405B parameters (dense). Released July 23, 2024. MMLU 5-shot: 87.3% (Instruct). 128K context. First "frontier-level" open model per Meta. HumanEval: 89.0% (0-shot). MATH: 73.8% (0-shot CoT). Llama 3.1 license. API pricing varies: Lambda $0.89/1M, Together $3.50/1M, Fireworks $3.00/1M, Bedrock $3.00/1M, Google $5.00/1M input, $16.00/1M output. Throughput: ~42 char/s. Trained on 15.6T+ tokens. Quantization support for FP8/INT8. Source: https://huggingface.co/meta-llama/Llama-3.1-405B-Instruct

Released July 23, 2024. MMLU 5-shot: 83.6% (Instruct). 128K context window. Llama 3.1 license. API pricing: ~$0.10-0.60/1M input, $0.30-0.60/1M output (varies widely by provider). HumanEval: 80.5% (0-shot). MMLU-Pro 5-shot CoT: 66.4%. Source: https://huggingface.co/meta-llama/Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct

Released July 23, 2024. Sizes: 8B, 70B, 405B. Major upgrade: 128K context window (16x Llama 3). Multilingual support. MMLU 5-shot: 69.4% (8B Instruct). Llama 3.1 license. API pricing: ~$0.02-0.03/1M input, $0.03-0.05/1M output. Tool use support. Source: https://huggingface.co/meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
123B parameters. Major improvement over Large 1. GPQA 48.6%, MMLU-Pro 69.7%, MATH-500 73.6%. Supports 80+ programming languages. Mistral Research License.
Audio-language model. Accepts audio+text input. Apache 2.0. ```
Specialized math models. First mathematical LLMs in Qwen family. Supports CoT (Chain-of-Thought). ```
Vision-language model. Naive Dynamic Resolution. MRoPE for multimodal alignment. ```
Major step forward in chat, coding, and reasoning. Tested on LMSYS as "sus-column-r." Third place on Chatbot Arena at launch. Integrated FLUX.1 image generation. MMLU 87.5%, MATH 76.1%, MMMU 66.1%.
Smaller sibling of Grok-2. ~90% of capability at lower latency. Available via X Premium and API.
Achieved SOTA results in theorem proving via RLPAF (Reinforcement Learning with Proof Assistant Feedback) and RMaxTS algorithms. Significant advancement over V1. Paper published Aug 15, 2024.

Proprietary flagship. Language parsing, instruction execution, long-text processing improvements. Comparable to GPT-4 and Llama 3.1 405B. GLM-4V-Plus: first universal video understanding model API in China. MMLU: ~86.8% (per source). CogView-3-Plus and CogVideoX also released simultaneously.
12B parameter multimodal LLM. Supports vision and tool-use. Processes both images and text. Apache 2.0 license.

Combined strengths of DeepSeek-V2-0628 and DeepSeek-Coder-V2-0724 into unified assistant. 236B total, 21B activated. MMLU: 80.6%. Drop: 87.8. IF-Eval: 80.6. GPQA-Diamond: 41.3. Significant improvement over V2 in instruction following. Outperformed both parent models.
Data-grounded Gemma variant. Released September 12, 2024. 2B parameters. Designed for factual accuracy with data grounding. Source: https://ai.google.dev/gemma/docs/releases
Smaller, faster reasoning model. 64K max output. Strong at coding and math. Competitive with o1 on many STEM benchmarks at much lower cost.
First reasoning model. Uses chain-of-thought reasoning before responding. 32K max output tokens. MMLU 90.8% (5-shot). GPQA Diamond 73.0%.
API ID: mistral-small-2409. Improved small model with better instruction following.
Apache 2.0. MMLU-Pro ~13.3. Qwen Research license for some variants. ```
GSM8K: 67.9. Apache 2.0. ```
MMLU ~79.9. MATH: 76.5. Apache 2.0. ```
MMLU same as Llama-3-70B (~83). Apache 2.0. ```
MMLU-redux: 64.4. MATH: 65.9. Not Apache 2.0. ```

HumanEval: 72.8. Arena-Hard: 49.20. Apache 2.0. ```
MATH benchmark (TIR): 87.8 (72B), 85.3 (7B), 79.7 (1.5B). Supports CoT and TIR. ```

Released September 25, 2024. Multimodal (text + image input, text output). 128K context. API pricing: ~$0.16/1M input/output. First multimodal Llama. MMMU: ~60.3%. Source: https://huggingface.co/meta-llama/Llama-3.2-11B-Vision-Instruct

Released September 25, 2024 at Meta Connect. Edge/mobile-optimized sizes: 1B and 3B (text-only). Vision models: 11B and 90B (multimodal). 128K-131K context window for all sizes. Lightweight models for on-device deployment. API pricing 1B: ~$0.10/1M input/output. Source: https://huggingface.co/meta-llama/Llama-3.2-1B

Released September 25, 2024. 131K context. Text-only edge model. API pricing: $0.04/1M input, $0.08/1M output (Llamagate). Throughput: 172 tok/s. Source: https://cloudprice.net/models/meta-llama-3-2-3b

Released September 25, 2024. Largest multimodal Llama 3.2. 128K context. API pricing: ~$0.72/1M input/output. Includes text capabilities of Llama 3.1 70B. MMMU: ~68.8%. Not available in EU due to regulatory restrictions. Source: https://huggingface.co/meta-llama/Llama-3.2-90B-Vision-Instruct

Post-trained on Llama 3.1 70B. Released October 2024. Strong on alignment and instruction-following benchmarks. Key dataset: HelpSteer2. Part of NVIDIA's Llama Nemotron Collection. NVIDIA Open Model License. Source: https://www.interconnects.ai/p/why-nvidia-builds-open-models-with
Japanese-language Gemma 2 variants. Released October 3, 2024. Source: https://ai.google.dev/gemma/docs/releases
Compact 3B parameter model optimized for edge deployment on phones, laptops, IoT devices. Apache 2.0 license.
Larger edge model. Exceptional performance-to-price ratio. Launched October 2024 alongside Ministral 3B.
Speed/cost tier for Claude 3.5 generation. Improved reasoning and coding vs Haiku 3.
Updated version with computer use capability introduced. Improved coding over original 3.5 Sonnet.

End-to-end speech large language model. Can adjust tone and dialect. Simulates real emotional expressions, pauses, and breaths. Similar to GPT-4o's audio capabilities. Released alongside AutoGLM agent.
Specialized coding models. LiveCodeBench 2407-2409: 31.4% (32B-Instruct). CRUXEval-I: 56.5. MultiPL-E: 70.4. ```
API ID: mistral-large-2411. Updated version of Mistral Large 2 with improved capabilities. Pixtral Large released simultaneously.
Content moderation tool for identifying and managing harmful text content.
Frontier-class multimodal model. 124B parameters. 1B-parameter visual encoder. Mistral Research License for research; Commercial License for commercial use.
First reasoning model in Qwen family. Competes with o1-preview. AIME and MATH benchmarks outperform o1-preview. 32B parameters. Apache 2.0. ```
Full o1 model. 100K max output. Improved reasoning over preview. Added tool use (function calling), structured outputs, and vision. MMLU 92.4%.

Released December 6, 2024. Text-only instruction-tuned model. 70B parameters but achieves performance comparable to Llama 3.1 405B at much lower serving cost. 128K context. MMLU 5-shot CoT: ~86.0% (improved post-training over 3.1 70B). MMLU-Pro CoT: 66.4%. Trained on 15T+ tokens. Llama 3.3 license. API pricing: ~$0.04-0.23/1M input, $0.40/1M output. Trained on 39.3M GPU hours on H100. Net-zero emissions. Multilingual: EN, DE, FR, IT, PT, HI, ES, TH. Source: https://huggingface.co/meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct

4.5B activated parameters (LLM: 4.1B, VE: 0.4B). OCRBench: 811. DocVQA: 93.3. ChartQA: 86.0. InfoVQA: 78.1. TextVQA: 84.2. RefCOCO val: 95.1. Best OCR performance, competitive with GPT-4o on document tasks.
2.8B activated parameters. OCRBench: 834. DocVQA: 92.3. ChartQA: 84.5. InfoVQA: 75.8. TextVQA: 83.4. RefCOCO val: 93.9. Strong performance on document-centric tasks.
Advanced MoE vision-language model. 1.0B activated parameters (LLM: 0.57B, VE: part of 3B total). OCRBench: 809. DocVQA: 88.9. ChartQA: 81.0. InfoVQA: 66.1. RefCOCO val: 84.7.

Flagship 671B MoE model, 37B active. Trained on 14.8T tokens. Auxiliary-loss-free load balancing. Multi-Token Prediction (MTP). Training cost ~$5.58M over 2 months. HumanEval-Mul: 82.6%. LiveCodeBench: 40.5%. MATH-500: 90.2%. DROP: 91.6. Training on 14.8T tokens. MIT license. One of the most impactful open-source releases �?? directly challenged GPT-4o at fraction of cost.
Updated Codestral release. January 2025 version. 256K context.

Foundation model. 456B total parameters, 45.9B activated per token. 80 layers. Lightning Attention + Softmax Attention hybrid (7:1 ratio). 32 experts, top-2 routing. RoPE with base 10,000,000. Trained on 14.8T tokens. MIT license. First model to achieve 1M context via Lightning Attention. GPT-4/Claude-3.5 competitive performance.
Vision-language companion to Text-01. ViT module integrated. 512B vision-language tokens through four-stage training. Released alongside Text-01.

Landmark reasoning model. 671B MoE, 37B active. Rivaled OpenAI o1. MMLU: 90.8%. AIME 2024: 79.8%. MATH-500: 97.3%. Codeforces: 2029 rating (96.3 percentile). Multi-stage training: cold-start SFT �?? RL �?? rejection sampling �?? final RL. Visible chain-of-thought. Broader impact: wiped ~$1T from US markets, Nvidia dropped 17%. MIT license. Distillable to smaller models.
70B distilled variant. Strong performance comparable to much larger non-distilled models.
Distilled to Llama-8B base.
Distilled from R1 to Qwen-1.5B. Outperformed Qwen-32B-Preview on reasoning tasks. Demonstrated effective knowledge transfer from large teacher model.
14B distilled variant.
32B distilled variant. Groq deprecated this model Apr 2025, recommending qwen-qwq-32b instead.
7B distilled reasoning model. Strong math and coding performance relative to size.

Pure RL-based reasoning model �?? trained exclusively with reinforcement learning, no SFT cold start. Explored emergent reasoning capabilities including "aha moments." Achieved strong results but suffered from readability and language-mixing issues. Serves as foundation for R1. AIME 2024: ~71%. Part of the landmark Jan 20, 2025 release alongside R1.

First reasoning model from Moonshot AI. Claimed to match OpenAI o1 performance in math, coding, and multimodal reasoning. MATH-500: 96.2%, C-Eval: 88.3%, IFEval: 87.2%. Multimodal capabilities (text + image).
Lightweight variant. Input: RMB 0.3/1M. Cache: RMB 0.06/1M. Output: RMB 0.6/1M. ```
Long context variant. 256K context window. ```
Sparse MoE architecture. Performance equivalent to dense models with 7x activation params. 50% gross margin. Cache hits: RMB 0.16/1M. Exchange rate: 1 USD = 7.30 RMB (Jan 2025). ```
Vision-language. Visual agent capabilities. Qwen2.5-VL-7B-Instruct: $0.20/1M output. ```
1M context. Apache 2.0. ```
1M context window using dual chunk attention + YaRN. Technical report: 2501.15383. ```

Proprietary MoE model. Pretrained on 20T+ tokens. Arena-Hard: 89.4. LiveBench: 62.2. GPQA-Diamond: 60.1. LiveCodeBench: 38.7. Ranked #7 overall Chatbot Arena, #1 in math and coding. Surpasses DeepSeek-V3. ```
24B parameters. Apache 2.0 license. Designed for efficient deployment. MMLU 80.62% (5-shot). Positioned against GPT-4o Mini and Claude 3.5 Haiku.
Full o3 model. More capable than o3-mini. Strong performance on coding benchmarks (SWE-bench Verified 71.6%).
Cost-efficient reasoning model. Supports adjustable reasoning effort (low/medium/high). 100K max output. Superior to o1-mini at lower price.
o3-mini with high reasoning effort setting. Better results on complex tasks at cost of latency.

Released February 5, 2025 as GA. 1M token context. Multimodal (text, image, audio, video). SWE-bench Verified: 51.8% (per anotherwrapper.com). Throughput: 257 tok/s. Deprecated February 2026 (planned). Replaced by 2.5 Flash. Native image generation capability added March 2025. Pricing: $0.10/1M input (text/image/video), $0.40/1M output. Batch: $0.05/1M input, $0.20/1M output. Source: https://anotherwrapper.com/tools/llm-pricing/gemini-20-flash

Released February 5, 2025 as experimental. 2M token context window �?? largest among Gemini 2.0 models. Aimed at complex reasoning and coding. Replaced by Gemini 2.5 Pro. Pricing: experimental models were free-tier only. Source: https://www.vals.ai/models/google_gemini-2.0-pro-exp-02-05
Specialized model for Arabic language and South Asian languages. Retirement: 2025-09-30.

First dedicated reasoning model from xAI. Trained on Colossus supercluster (200K+ GPUs, 10x compute of Grok-2). "Think" mode for chain-of-thought reasoning. DeepSearch for agentic web scanning. AIME 93%, GPQA competitive. Knowledge cutoff November 2024. Chatbot Arena Elo ~1402.
High-speed variant of Grok-3. Optimized for enterprise tasks.
Lightweight reasoning model. 10x cheaper than Grok-3. Good for simpler reasoning tasks.
Fast variant of Grok-3-mini. Higher throughput for latency-sensitive applications.

First Claude with explicit chain-of-thought reasoning (extended thinking mode). Reasoning model �?? additional "extended thinking mode" when turned on allows Claude to think before answering. Max output 64K tokens. Deprecating 2026-05-05.

Released February 25, 2025. GA status. Optimized for lowest latency and cost in the 2.0 family. 1M token context. Superseded by 2.5 Flash-Lite. Source: changelog shows "Released gemini-2.0-flash-lite, Gemini 2.0 Flash-Lite's GA" on Feb 25, 2025.

Research preview. Largest, most expensive OpenAI model. Unsupervised learning scaled up. API access removed July 14, 2025. ChatGPT Pro users can still access under "Legacy Models." Pricing was $75/$150 (later reduced to $60/$120 for some tiers).

Hybrid Mamba-Transformer models. Released March-June 2025. Sizes: 8B, 47B, 4B-128K. First hybrid architecture from NVIDIA for inference efficiency. Research release. Source: https://www.interconnects.ai/p/why-nvidia-builds-open-models-with
Most capable reasoning model. Uses more compute for deeper reasoning. Available via API and ChatGPT Pro. 10x more expensive than o1.
Expanded context to 131K. Matches DeepSeek-R1-671B on math/coding. Based on Qwen2.5-32B. Trained with RL on outcome-based rewards. Apache 2.0. ```

16.8B parameters, 2.75B activated (MoE). MMLU-Redux: 65.67 (Base), 70.35 (Instruct). MMLU-Pro: 41.47 (Base), 49.19 (Instruct). Arena-Hard: 42.09 (Instruct). Comparable to Qwen2.5-7B. Apache 2.0 / MIT license. ```
290B parameters, 28.8B activated (MoE). MMLU-Redux: 83.90. MMLU-Pro: 67.57. GPQA: 43.81. Arena-Hard: 74.25. Comparable to DeepSeek-V2.5 and Qwen2.5-72B. BFCL-v2 tool use: 74.90%. MIT license. ```

Released March 10, 2025. 12B parameters. Vision-capable. 128K context. MMLU ~72.0% (estimated). Outperforms Llama-4-5B and o3-mini on some benchmarks per Google claims. Source: https://www.thehindu.com/sci-tech/technology/google-releases-gemma-3-its-most-advanced-compact-ai-model/article69324750.ece

Third generation Gemma. Released March 10, 2025. 1B, 4B, 12B, 27B sizes. First vision-capable Gemma (4B+). 128K context window on larger variants. Multilingual (35+ languages). MMLU varies by size. Built on Gemini 2.0 technology. Source: https://www.thehindu.com/sci-tech/technology/google-releases-gemma-3-its-most-advanced-compact-ai-model/article69324750.ece

Largest Gemma 3 variant. Released March 10, 2025. 27B parameters. Vision-capable. 128K context. MMLU ~78.5% (estimated). Competes with larger open models on single-GPU deployments. Source: https://ai.google.dev/gemma/docs/releases
Released March 10, 2025. 4B parameters. Vision-capable (first in Gemma family). 128K context. MMLU ~62.0% (estimated from comparable sizes). Source: https://ai.google.dev/gemma/docs/releases

Safety classifier model. Released alongside Gemma 3 (March 10, 2025). Filters sexually explicit, dangerous, and violent images. Initial ShieldGemma released July 31, 2024. Source: https://ai.google.dev/gemma/docs/releases
24B parameters. Enhanced text performance, multimodal processing. Image understanding capabilities. Apache 2.0 license.

Post-trained from Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct. Released March 18, 2025. 49B parameters (NAS-distilled from 70B). 128K context. Reasoning model. Fits on single H200 GPU at high workloads. Nearly matches Qwen2-VL-72B performance. Apache 2.0. ```

Updated V3 with improved tool reasoning and reduced language mixing. Released as V3-0324 on HuggingFace. Addressed user feedback about Chinese words in English responses. SWE-bench Verified: 54.8% (CAISI). MMLU-Pro: 89.0%. GPQA: 79.3%.

16B parameter MoE vision-language model (3B active). Open-source under MIT license. Kimi-VL-Thinking variant released June 2025 for reasoning.

Released April 5, 2025. MoE: 17B active, 400B total, 128 experts. 1M token context. Multimodal. MMLU 5-shot: 85.5%. MMLU-Pro: 62.9% (Meta) / 80.5% (other evals). SWE-bench Verified: 48.7%. GPQA Diamond: 69.8%. MATH-500: 89.3%. HumanEval: 91.2%. Fits on single H100 host. Llama 4 license. API pricing: ~$0.19-0.50/1M input/output (varies by provider). Released April 5, 2025. MoE architecture: 17B active parameters, 109B total, 16 experts. 10M token context window (industry-leading at release). Multimodal (text + image input). MMLU 5-shot: 79.6%. SWE-bench Verified: 32.8%. MMLU-Pro: 74.3%. GPQA Diamond: 57.2%. Fits on single H100 GPU (INT4). Llama 4 license. API pricing: ~$0.08/1M input, $0.30/1M output (DeepInfra). Knowledge cutoff: August 2024. ~40T training tokens. Not available in EU. Source: https://build.nvidia.com/meta/llama-4-scout-17b-16e-instruct/modelcard

Dense Transformer (NOT MoE) �?? all 253B parameters active. Released April 7, 2025. Post-trained from Llama-3.1-405B-Instruct. 128K context window (131K reported by some sources). Reasoning model with "detailed thinking on/off" toggle via system prompt. First open-weight model to beat DeepSeek R1 on GPQA Diamond and LiveCodeBench. Fits on single 8xH100 node (FP8). Neural Architecture Search (NAS) compressed from 405B. Multi-phase post-training: SFT (Math, Code, Reasoning, Chat, Tool Calling) + GRPO RL. MMLU: ~88.0% (estimated). API pricing: $0.60/1M input, $1.80/1M output. Trained November 2024�??April 2025. Source: https://build.nvidia.com/nvidia/llama-3_1-nemotron-ultra-253b-v1/modelcard
Million-token context window. Three variants: gpt-4.1, gpt-4.1-mini ($0.40/$1.60), gpt-4.1-nano ($0.10/$0.40). Improved coding (SWE-bench Verified 54.6%). Replaced GPT-4o for most use cases.
Balanced cost-performance variant of GPT-4.1. Same 1M context.
Fastest, cheapest GPT-4.1 variant. Designed for simple tasks and high-volume applications.

32B parameter series. Enhanced for agent capabilities: tool usage, web search, code generation. Real-time code generation for HTML/CSS/JS/SVG. MIT license. Released simultaneously with IPO filing announcement. Includes GLM-Z1 inference models. 8x faster inference than DeepSeek-R1 on consumer GPUs via optimized GQA, quantization, speculative sampling.
Compact 9B variant of 0414 series. MIT license. Strong math and general tasks for resource-constrained environments.
Inference-optimized variant. 200 tokens/sec on consumer GPUs. Part of 32B-0414 open-source release.
"Rumination" reasoning model. Can search internet, use tools, conduct deep analysis, self-verify. Autonomous agent capabilities for complex open-ended queries. Step beyond purely reactive AI.
Upgraded Ling-Lite. Code and math improvements over 0220 version. ```
Successor to o3-mini. Improved reasoning with image inputs. Supports reasoning effort parameter. Released April 2025.
32B multimodal model. MoE architecture. OCR, captioning, spatial analysis. Open-sourced under MIT license.
Multimodal vision model. 9B variant. Released Apr 25, 2025.

MoE. 30B total, 3B active. 128K context. MMLU-Pro: 82.2. Apache 2.0. ```
Dense. 128K context. MMLU-Pro ~82.2. Apache 2.0. ```

Part of Llama Nemotron Collection. Efficient small model for edge deployment. Released May 2025. 8B parameters. Post-trained from Llama 3.1 8B. NVIDIA Open Model License. Source: https://www.interconnects.ai/p/why-nvidia-builds-open-models-with

Medical-domain Gemma. Released May 20, 2025. 4B and 27B sizes. MedGemma 27B multimodal released July 9, 2025. MedGemma 1.5 (4B) released January 13, 2026. Source: https://ai.google.dev/gemma/docs/releases
24B parameters. Open-source SWE agent model. Designed for full agentic software development. Outperformed GPT-4.1-mini on SWE benchmarks by 20%+. Apache 2.0.

Claude 4 generation launch. Major coding gains, SWE-bench leadership at 72.5%. Extended thinking capabilities. 1M token context window in public beta (Aug 2025). Retiring June 15, 2026.
Balanced model in Claude 4 family. Extended thinking capabilities. 1M token context beta from August 2025. Improved "computer use" support. Retiring June 15, 2026.
